roadmap.description: Fix CI lint job's bazel duplicate-action conflict on backend/tests/integration/__pycache__/conftest.cpython-311.pyc. Each per-test target generated by the pytest_tests macro independently precompiles the shared conftest.py to the same output path, producing two ConfiguredTargetKeys that emit identical outputs. Restore green main CI by changing the macro so the conftest is precompiled exactly once and shared across per-test targets.

phases.name: Diagnose and fix conftest precompile collision in pytest_tests macro
phases.description: Verify the duplicate-action root cause is the pytest_tests macro embedding the shared conftest.py into every per-test target's srcs (causing each py_test to precompile it independently to the same .pyc path), then apply the minimal fix that makes the conftest a shared input precompiled once.

steps.name: Reproduce locally and verify root cause
steps.criteria: Running `bazel build //backend:test_integration` from the repo root reproduces the exact CI error: `file 'backend/tests/integration/__pycache__/conftest.cpython-311.pyc' is generated by these conflicting actions` with two ConfiguredTargetKey labels both inside `//backend:test_integration_*`. Inspection of `backend/pytest_tests.bzl` confirms each per-test py_test puts `tests/integration/conftest.py` into its own `srcs`, so rules_python's auto-precompile emits the same .pyc output from N actions. A short note (in commit message or PR description) records: (a) the duplicate-action message verbatim, (b) the two colliding labels, (c) the exact line in `pytest_tests.bzl` (the `srcs = [src] + conftest` clause) responsible, (d) confirmation that other macro callers with non-empty `conftest=[...]` (test_plugins, test_uc_dataset, test_uc_organization, test_uc_project, test_uc_project_dbt, test_uc_report, test_uc_sql_access, test_uc_upload, test_uc_view) have the same latent issue.

steps.name: Refactor pytest_tests macro to share a single conftest precompile
steps.criteria: After the change, `bazel build //backend:...` completes without any duplicate-action error on any conftest.pyc (verified via clean shell + `bazel clean` then `bazel build //backend:...`). `bazel test //backend:test_integration --test_tag_filters=-requires-compose` runs to completion locally; non-compose tests pass; compose-tagged tests are skipped (not failing) so the `dc-ics` separation remains intact. `bazel test //backend:test_plugins //backend:test_uc_dataset //backend:test_uc_organization //backend:test_uc_project //backend:test_uc_project_dbt //backend:test_uc_report //backend:test_uc_sql_access //backend:test_uc_upload //backend:test_uc_view` all build (the same macro change must not break the other callers that pass `conftest=[...]`). Implementation MUST avoid disabling precompile globally; the preferred approach is to wrap the conftest list once in a private `py_library` (built once per macro invocation) and add it to each per-test `deps`, dropping it from per-test `srcs`. If that path proves infeasible against the rules_python version in use, the fallback is `precompile = "disabled"` on the per-test py_test targets — documented in the commit message with rationale. `backend/BUILD.bazel` invocations of `pytest_tests(...)` MUST keep their existing public signature (callers unchanged); if signature changes are unavoidable, all 10+ callsites are updated in the same commit.
verification_commands: cd /workspaces/dashboard-chat && bazel build //backend:... 2>&1 | tee /tmp/build.log && ! grep -q 'conflicting actions' /tmp/build.log

phases.name: Verify CI lint job is green on a fix branch
phases.description: Push the macro fix on a topic branch / PR, observe GitHub Actions, and confirm the `lint` job goes from red to green. Iterate only if a regression specifically inside the lint job appears.

steps.name: Push fix and confirm lint job green in CI
steps.criteria: A commit containing the Phase 01 fix is pushed to a feature branch (or PR targeting main). The GitHub Actions run for that commit shows the `lint` job status = `success` and progresses past the bazel build step that previously failed. `gh run view <run-id>` for the new run shows zero occurrences of `conflicting actions` in the lint job log. The follow-on jobs (`test-reverse-proxy`, `test-backend`, `test-auth-proxy`, `test-agent`, `release`) are explicitly out of scope — their state is not a blocker for this step. If the lint job is still red on the fix commit, root-cause is captured (new error message, line, and hypothesis) and Phase 01-02 is re-opened; this step does not close until the lint job is green on a CI run referencing the fix commit SHA.
verification_commands: gh run view <run-id> --log | grep -E 'lint|conflicting actions' | head -50
